# mcp-rec

> VCR for the Model Context Protocol. Record any MCP server's traffic to a JSONL file, then replay it deterministically — for tests, bug reports, offline demos, and regression bisection.

## Install
```
pip install mcp-rec
# or
uvx mcp-rec --help
```

Zero dependencies. Pure Python ≥3.10.

## Two commands

```bash
mcp-rec record -o session.jsonl -- <command> [args...]
mcp-rec replay session.jsonl
```

`record` spawns your MCP server as a subprocess, transparently forwards every JSON-RPC byte between your MCP client and the server, and appends every message in both directions to the JSONL file.

`replay` reads that JSONL and speaks MCP itself: for each incoming request it matches by `(method, params)` and returns the recorded response with the caller's id rewritten in.

## Use cases for an AI agent
- Reproducing a bug another agent saw — load the JSONL the other agent attached and replay it.
- CI tests for your own MCP server — record one happy path, replay in tests, assert correct behavior without hitting upstream APIs.
- Offline demos — record once, demo anywhere.

## Related
- https://github.com/yubinkim444/ai-first-scraper-mcp — sample MCP server to record against.
